Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Defamation Per Se, Defamation Per Quod, Trade Libel Claims And Factually Associated Claims In Plaintiff'S Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-27-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

BRITTANY S. ARMSTRONG WHITTINGTON, Esq., for Defendants.

RE: Defendant's Special Motion To Strike Defamation Per Se, Defamation Per Quod, Trade Libel Claims And Factually Associated Claims In Plaintiff's Complaint.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The motion fails to comply with Cal. Rules of Court ("CRC") 3.1113(d). Defendant's opening brief exceeds the page requirements of the CRC by eleven pages. Defendant may renew this motion by filing a rule-compliant motion or obtaining leave of court to file an oversized brief. (See CRC 3.1113(g) [court may treat oversized brief as late-filed]; CRC 3.1300(d) [court may refuse to consider late-filed brief].)

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
